{"query":"Kamala Harris new political organization name founded 2025","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1643,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://www.politico.com/news/2025/01/23/kamala-harris-pioneer49-future-00200305","title":"Kamala Harris provides a big signal about her next move","content":"The new organization will be a vehicle to help her decide her next steps."},{"url":"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kamala_Harris","title":"Kamala Harris - Wikipedia","content":"KAH-mə-lə DAY-vee; born October 20, 1964) is an American politician and attorney who served as the 49th vice president of the United States from 2021 to 2025 under President Joe Biden.
When her vice presidency began, Harris presided over an evenly split U.S.
She cast 33 tie-breaking votes, more than any other vice president, including votes to pass the American Rescue Plan Act of 2021 and the Inflation Reduction Act.
Kamala Harris attended Vanier College in Montreal in 1981–1982; she then attended Howard University, a historically black university in Washington, D.C.
San Francisco district attorney (2002–2011)
In the September and October Brett Kavanaugh Supreme Court confirmation hearings, Harris questioned Brett Kavanaugh about a meeting he may have had regarding the Mueller Investigation with a member of Kasowitz Benson Torres, the law firm founded by Donald Trump's personal attorney, Marc Kasowitz.
More than 20,000 people attended her campaign launch event in her hometown of Oakland, California, on January 27, according to a police estimate.
Some speculated that Harris would run in the 2026 California gubernatorial election; in July 2025, she announced she would not.
In 2001, Harris briefly dated talk show host Montel Williams.
Harris, Kamala (September 23, 2025).
"Kamala Harris wins attorney general's race as Steve Cooley concedes".
Kamala Harris speaks out against Betsy DeVos as part of Democrats' 24-hour blitz on Senate floor".
"Kamala Harris says voter suppression kept Stacey Abrams, Andrew Gillum out of office."},{"url":"https://www.ms.now/news/kamala-harris-campaign-social-media-trump-2026","title":"Kamala Harris gives campaign accounts new life in fight against Trump","content":"Harris’s campaign pages on TikTok and X, which have remained inactive since her November defeat to Donald Trump, are being transformed into a progressive advocacy tool through a partnership with People For the American Way.
The rebranded accounts, now called “Headquarters,” retain the millions of followers accumulated during the campaign.
Vice President Harris and People For the American Way revealed the new venture Thursday, describing it as an effort “to funnel young people into both online and offline action for progressive values.”
Her senior adviser Kirsten Allen will join the organization’s board of directors.
People For the American Way, which has roughly 1 million members, plans to expand the operation to YouTube and Substack, developing long-form video content alongside rapid-response social media posts.
Trump’s “War Room” campaign account on X has remained active since the election, continuing to shape political narratives online."},{"url":"https://www.foxnews.com/politics/out-office-back-online-harris-rebrands-kamala-hq-2026-midterms-amid-2028-buzz","title":"Former VP Kamala Harris revives Kamala HQ platform amid 2028 speculation | Fox News","content":"In a move sure to spark more 2028 speculation, former Vice President Kamala Harris on Thursday relaunched her Kamala HQ social media presence used during her 2024 White House run as "an online organizing project for next-generation campaigning.""}]}
